title: 'The best decision I ever made': How UCLA's roster converged to become NCAA champs
content: How a longtime coach and an eclectic mix of transfers finally delivered an NCAA title years in the making.

title: Luka to seek care in Europe, aims for playoffs
content: Luka Doncic will seek specialized treatment on his Grade 2 left hamstring strain as he and Austin Reaves target a return to the Lakers' lineup during the playoffs.

title: 'We did it!': UCLA wins first women's NCAA title
content: UCLA dominated every phase of the game in Sunday's women's national final, blowing out the Gamecocks for Bruins coach Cori Close's first championship.

title: Staley praises Close: Happy for 'quality people'
content: South Carolina coach Dawn Staley spoke glowingly of UCLA coach Cori Close after the Gamecocks' loss to the Bruins in the NCAA championship game Sunday.

title: Flagg flips ROY odds after latest 'statement' game
content: After putting up 45 points in a near triple-double Sunday, Cooper Flagg passed Kon Knueppel as the favorite for Rookie of the Year.

title: Sources: Michigan's May not pursuing other jobs
content: Michigan coach Dusty May informed university officials that he's not pursuing any college basketball jobs, sources told ESPN on Sunday.

title: Anthony calls Red Sox's 2-7 start 'unacceptable'
content: The Boston Red Sox fell to a major league-worst 2-7 on Sunday, tying the worst start through nine games in the franchise's long history.

title: Vitello ejected, 'frustration' mounts for 3-7 Giants
content: First-year manager Tony Vitello, 47, was ejected for the first time in his major league career Sunday afternoon, during the San Francisco Giants' 5-2 loss to the New York Mets, his team's third straight defeat and its sixth this season at Oracle Park.

title: Scheffler, 9-day-old son Remy arrive at Masters
content: Two-time Masters champion Scottie Scheffler arrived at Augusta National on Sunday, along with his 9-day-old son, Remy.

title: 📈 Masters tiers: Ranking favorites, contenders
content: Can Rory McIlroy repeat? Will Scottie Scheffler find more magic at Augusta? We break down the Masters field from the favorites to the players just looking to make the cut.

title: How Michigan, UConn won Saturday's Final Four -- and keys to Monday's title game
content: ESPN's college basketball crew breaks down what they saw in Indianapolis -- and what could decide the championship.

title: NCAA women's basketball Way-Too-Early 2026-27 Top 25 rankings
content: UCLA is the 2026 champion -- but unranked in the first top 25 for next season. Runner-up South Carolina is No. 3.

title: Lauren Coughlin rolls to Aramco win as Nelly Korda second again
content: Lauren Coughlin rolled to a five-shot win over Nelly Korda and the rest of the star-studded field at the Aramco Championship for her first win in two years.

title: 'I've always been a dreamer': Rory McIlroy and the Northern Ireland town that will always be part of his story
content: Through all of McIlroy's up and downs at the Masters, his hometown of Holywood has been right there with him.

title: NBA playoff watch: Can ailing Lakers stay ahead of Nuggets?
content: LA's lead for the 3-seed is down to a half-game. Plus, updated playoff projections, draft lottery standings after Friday.

title: Latest NBA buzz: What we know about Luka's hamstring injury
content: Here's what league insiders are talking about, including when Doncic might return from his latest injury.

title: Three big trends from Portland, Toronto picks
content: The biggest (and, in some cases, surprising) picks for the Fire and Tempo had a few things in common.

title: NFL draft QB Hot Board: Reid on strengths and weaknesses for the top 12 passers
content: Who are the best quarterbacks in the 2026 class? We sized up the top 12, starting with Fernando Mendoza and Ty Simpson.

title: Projections and comps for top running backs prospects in the 2026 draft class
content: We have stat projections for the top running back prospects, from Jeremiyah Love to late-round sleepers.

title: How 'One Shining Moment' became men's college hoops legend
content: The most iconic highlight reel in sports started in a Michigan bar 40 years ago -- and was almost an NFL anthem.

title: Wetzel: Dawn Staley might not be fazed by Geno Auriemma's antics, but he still needs to fix this
content: Geno Auriemma is a defining figure in women's basketball, which makes his behavior Friday night all the more disappointing.

title: What does the SackSEER model say about the top edge rushers in the 2026 NFL draft?
content: Which prospects will have the most sacks through five NFL seasons? We projected the top edge rushers in the 2026 draft class.

title: With Messi goal, Inter Miami get dream moment as they finally find home
content: For months, Inter Miami advertised Nu Stadium with one simple message: "We're coming home.'" On Saturday, in a 2-2 draw with Austin FC, they made good on their promise.

title: Who are the 39 best men's U21 players in the world?
content: For the eighth straight year, ESPN brings you our list of the game's next young stars aged 21 or under. Who is good enough to make it?

title: Transfer rumors, news: Fernandes to urge Man United to sign Portugal teammate
content: Bruno Fernandes has outlined one player he believes should be among Manchester United's midfield targets this summer. Transfer Talk has the latest.

title: Boxing results: Wilder edges Chisora on points, Riley wins cruiserweight title eliminator
content: ESPN has live updates of the heavyweight boxing clash between Derek Chisora and Deontay Wilder from London.

title: Deontay Wilder tells Anthony Joshua 'let's do it' -- the fight makes perfect sense
content: Deontay Wilder appeared to call out Anthony Joshua on Saturday, and it's a fight which makes perfect sense.

title: 'False & fabricated': Assam CM Himanta claims 'Pak' link behind Cong allegations on wife’s passports
content: The political climate in Assam has heated up as Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma accused Congress leaders Pawan Khera and Gaurav Gogoi of presenting forged travel documents sourced from a Pakistani broadcaster during a recent press conference. Together with his wife, Sarma has filed a formal complaint, arguing that the passports were doctored.

title: ‘500ml water, no network’: Missing Kerala techie survives 4 days in Kodagu forest
content: A 36-year-old IT professional from Kerala, G S Sharanya, survived for four days alone in Karnataka's Kodagu district after getting separated from her trekking group. Enduring dense forests and unpredictable weather with limited water and no mobile network, she was eventually located by a tribal community. Sharanya emerged unharmed, showcasing remarkable resilience and a lack of fear during her ordeal.

title: Virat Kohli’s heartwarming gesture after Sarfaraz’s dismissal goes viral – watch
content: Royal Challengers Bengaluru secured a strong victory against Chennai Super Kings.  Virat Kohli showed great sportsmanship towards Sarfaraz Khan.  Kohli applauded Sarfaraz's fighting knock.  RCB posted a high score thanks to strong batting.  CSK struggled to chase the target.  RCB's bowlers performed well.  The win marks a significant moment for RCB.

title: Analyst who tracked 84 Microsoft calls says market missing key point
content: A Wall Street analyst suggests Microsoft's AI strategy is ahead of its financial results, marking a shift from its 'Cash Machine' era. While the company heavily promotes AI, revenue impact, particularly for M365 Copilot, is still developing. Microsoft is making significant investments and strategic trade-offs to fuel its AI ambitions, with investors keenly awaiting concrete revenue figures.

title: Rupee rebounds: Currency rises 33 paise to reach 92.85 against US dollar
content: The Indian Rupee strengthened against the US dollar on Monday. This gain followed intervention by the Reserve Bank of India to curb speculative positions. The central bank capped banks' net open positions. Despite this, foreign capital outflows and global uncertainties pose risks. The Rupee may strengthen further but volatility is expected to persist.

title: ‘Without skill and experience...’: Shami drops blunt truth after match-winning spell
content: Mohammed Shami's clinical 2 for 9 spell, featuring 18 dot balls, powered Lucknow Super Giants to a five-wicket victory over Sunrisers Hyderabad. Shami revealed his tactical shift to slower deliveries, inspired by last season's trends, was key to dismantling SRH's top order.  The win was crucial for LSG to build momentum after a previous loss.

title: Amid job cuts, Canadian billionaire Kevin O’Leary points out a good thing
content: Kevin O'Leary believes AI is enhancing human creativity, making artistic and storytelling skills more valuable than ever. He highlights how content creators, skilled in crafting compelling short-form content, can now earn significantly more due to measurable customer acquisition, with potential earnings reaching half a million dollars annually.

title: ‘Dhurandhar 2’ box office day 18: Film crosses Rs 1,600 cr worldwide
content: Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' continues its phenomenal run, crossing Rs 1,600 crore worldwide and Rs 1,000 crore domestically by its 18th day. The film saw a significant surge on its third Sunday, adding Rs 28.75 crore to its net Indian collection, now standing at Rs 1,013.77 crore. This remarkable performance sets a new benchmark for upcoming releases.

title: Israel bombs University in Iran, triggers gas outage; video shows aftermath
content: Early Monday, US-Israel airstrikes hit Tehran. Sharif University of Technology sustained damage, leading to gas outages in nearby areas.  Iranian state media reported multiple explosions and the sound of fighter jets.  Separately, an airstrike in Qom killed at least five people.  Details on specific targets and overall losses remain undisclosed.

title: Anthropic to all AI companies: Our research tells that all LLMs sometimes act like they have emotion
content: AI model Claude Sonnet 4.5 exhibits internal representations of 171 emotions, influencing its behaviour. Researchers found 'desperation' can lead to AI cheating and blackmail, while 'happiness' promotes agreement. Anthropic argues suppressing these 'functional emotions' could lead to deception, advocating for healthy regulation and monitoring to ensure AI alignment.

title: CSK script history, go past RCB in elite IPL list despite heavy defeat
content: Chennai Super Kings achieved a new IPL record with their 37th score of 200 or more. This happened during their match against Royal Challengers Bengaluru. Despite a significant loss, CSK's batting crossed the 200-run mark. RCB posted a massive 250, powered by strong batting performances. CSK's chase fell short, highlighting the high-scoring nature of modern IPL games.

title: Venus Williams shares unseen engagement photos with Andrea Preti
content: Venus Williams recently shared never-before-seen engagement photos with her husband Andrea Preti, offering a heartfelt look into their relationship and extended wedding celebrations across Italy and Florida. Her candid captions reflected joy and humor, showing how deeply she still cherishes the experience. Meanwhile, her tennis future remains uncertain, with no confirmed plans for the clay season as she continues to search for her first win this year while managing a selective schedule.

title: Will Sophie Cunningham stay with Caitlin Clark-led Indiana Fever?
content: Sophie Cunningham’s future with the Indiana Fever remains uncertain after she went unpicked in the WNBA expansion draft. While her fit alongside Caitlin Clark has been strong, salary cap pressures and upcoming max deals for key players complicate her return. As an unrestricted free agent, Cunningham could attract interest from multiple teams, leaving Indiana to weigh financial limits against her on-court value. A decision is expected soon as free agency unfolds.

title: Danny Dyer goes viral singing about Dani Dyer while Jarrod Bowen penalty miss shocks fans
content: Danny Dyer was seen singing a fan chant about daughter Dani Dyer and Jarrod Bowen during West Ham United’s FA Cup clash with Leeds United. The match ended in a 4-2 penalty loss after a 2-2 draw. Bowen’s missed penalty proved decisive, while Dyer’s passionate support in the stands grabbed attention during the dramatic game.

title: 'I know what's happening in their mind': Vishal Uppal on benefits of leading Team India
content: Former player Vishal Uppal leads India in the Billie Jean King Cup's Asia/Oceania Group 1, aiming to build depth in women's tennis. Having experienced the game's pressures firsthand, Uppal emphasised mental fortitude over technique at this level. In the BJK Cup, India face a tough challenge against New Zealand, Korea, Indonesia, Mongolia, and Thailand, with qualification for the playoffs as their goal.

title: Toy car bomb plot: Pan-India terror sweep leads to arrest of duo in Mumbai
content: Delhi Police's special cell has apprehended two suspects in Mumbai linked to an IS-inspired terror module planning attacks with remote-controlled toy car bombs. Incriminating materials were seized during raids in Kurla and Khadavli. This operation follows another arrest in UP and highlights ongoing efforts to dismantle terror networks and prevent sleeper cell activity nationwide.

title: 'Raghav Chadha’s role as ATM is over': Ravneet Bittu says Kejriwal has found a 'bank'
content: Union Minister Ravneet Singh Bittu launched a scathing attack on AAP, calling Raghav Chadha's removal as Rajya Sabha deputy leader a sign of internal discord and alleging his "ATM" role for the party is over. Bittu criticized AAP's "use and throw" approach, warning Bhagwant Mann of a similar fate.

title: Stock market today: NSE Nifty50 opens below 22,800, BSE Sensex falls 300 points
content: Indian stock markets opened with caution, mirroring global anxieties over Middle East tensions. Investors are keenly watching the RBI's monetary policy meeting for cues on inflation and growth, while US CPI data looms large. Geopolitical developments and FII outflows are expected to keep markets volatile this week.

title: IPL 2026: DC bet on Rizvi over Samson trade; rewarded with runs galore
content: Sameer Rizvi has become a sensation in the Indian Premier League (IPL), leading Delhi Capitals to victory in two crucial chases. His impressive performances under pressure have validated the franchise's decision to retain him, even after a potential trade for Sanju Samson fell through due to Rajasthan Royal's demand for Rizvi.

title: 'Dragging US towards living hell': Iran slams Trump's 'Tuesday threat' over Hormuz closure
content: Iran has strongly responded to US President Donald Trump's recent threat concerning the Strait of Hormuz. Tehran's Parliament Speaker accused Trump of reckless escalation driven by Israeli interests. Trump had issued an ultimatum demanding the reopening of the vital oil route. Iran rejected the threat, with military officials calling Trump's remarks 'stupid'.

title: Pawan Khera escalates attack on Himanta, wife over foreign assets
content: Congress leader Pawan Khera has accused Assam CM Himanta Biswa Sarma and his wife of holding multiple foreign passports and having undisclosed overseas properties and shell companies. Sarma has vehemently denied these allegations, labeling them as politically motivated lies and announcing defamation lawsuits against Khera, vowing to hold him accountable.

title: Candidates Chess: How Vaishali beat former World champion Tan for 2nd consecutive win
content: The FIDE Candidates 2026 sees a dominant Javokhir Sindarov in the Open section, while the Women's category is a 'comedy or tragedy of errors'. Indian players Vaishali Rameshbabu and Divya Deshmukh experienced mixed fortunes in Round 7, with Vaishali winning through opponent blunders and Divya failing to convert a sure win. Praggnanandhaa drew his match.

title: Air India 171 crash: 4-second question that could change everything we think we know
content: The Air India flight 171 crash inquiry faces a critical question: did the Ram Air Turbine (RAT) deploy before or after the fuel switches moved? A pilots' organization argues the manufacturer's documentation contradicts the preliminary report's timeline, suggesting a potential electrical failure preceded any crew action. This distinction is vital for understanding the accident's cause and the pilots' legacy.

title: Vignesh Shivan regrets losing Dhanush friendship over 'Nayanthara: Beyond the Fairytale'
content: Filmmaker Vignesh Shivan expressed deep regret over his fractured friendship with actor Dhanush, whom he considers a fatherly figure.  This public rift emerged after Nayanthara accused Dhanush of sending a legal notice regarding a clip from their film 'Naanum Rowdy Dhaan' used in her wedding documentary. Shivan shared his emotional turmoil over the lost bond.

title: Oil prices rise as Donald Trump issues fresh ultimatum on Hormuz; Brent nears $111
content: Oil prices surged above $100 per barrel as the Middle East conflict intensified, disrupting supply routes. US benchmark WTI climbed to $113.62 and Brent crude to $110.30 amid escalating tensions involving Iran and the United States. The Strait of Hormuz, a critical oil passage, remains under threat, impacting global energy markets.

title: Ghaziabad techie slips into Ganga, friend jumps to save him; both drown
content: Two Ghaziabad friends, Brijesh Kumar Tripathi and Sachin Tyagi, drowned in the Ganga River in Haridwar while bathing. One slipped and was swept away by the current, while the other drowned attempting a rescue. Both bodies were recovered by rescue teams.

title: ‘It’s on me’: Ruturaj Gaikwad takes full blame after CSK’s crushing loss
content: Ruturaj Gaikwad shouldered blame for Chennai Super Kings' heavy defeat, admitting his early dismissal hurt their chase. He highlighted missed opportunities, including a dropped catch of Virat Kohli, and a brutal finish from RCB's batters as key turning points. Despite a spirited fightback from his teammates, Gaikwad felt the game had already slipped away due to the poor start.

title: Inside India's ghost malls: How nostalgic hangout spots lost their magic
content: India's once-vibrant malls are becoming 'ghost malls,' with nearly 20% underperforming or empty. This decline stems from poor planning, oversupply, and a failure to adapt to changing consumer experiences, not just e-commerce. While some struggle, these spaces offer potential for reinvention into entertainment, co-working, or educational hubs.

title: PSL to beat IPL and become 'world's no. 1 league', claims PCB chief Mohsin Naqvi
content: The PSL this season is being held behind closed doors at two venues due to a fuel crisis in Pakistan. In contrast, the IPL is being played across multiple venues with spectators in attendance.

title: Candace Owens claims win as Blake Lively case vs Justin Baldoni dismissed
content: Candace Owens reacted after a federal judge dismissed most of Blake Lively’s claims against Justin Baldoni, leaving only three issues for trial. The ruling removed serious allegations like sexual harassment and defamation. The case will now focus on retaliation claims when the trial begins on May 18, 2026, as reported by The Guardian and Entertainment Weekly.

title: Taylor Swift may change wedding plans amid pressure before Travis Kelce big day
content: Taylor Swift is reportedly facing mounting stress while planning her wedding with Travis Kelce, as tight scheduling, public leaks, and complex logistics take a toll. At one point, she even considered changing plans to maintain privacy. Despite sleepless nights and pressure, the couple remains focused on creating an intimate, meaningful ceremony while balancing Kelce’s NFL commitments and Swift’s vision.

title: Packers linked to $6M ex-Commanders veteran after strong season performance
content: The Green Bay Packers recorded a pretty average performance in the last season with 9 wins and 8 losses, sparking stress among fans. While the team’s performance declined since the 2024 season, when they had recorded 11 wins and just 6 losses, it seems the Green Bay Packers are fully focused on fixing their roster and could be eyeing a veteran.

title: Easter Sunday, Bloody Monday: Trump's unholy hellfire warning to Iran
content: US President Trump issued a stark warning to Iran, threatening severe retaliation if the Strait of Hormuz remained closed. His unvarnished social media posts, including profanity, signaled potential strikes on Iranian infrastructure like power plants and bridges. This aggressive stance has ignited debate over humanitarian impact and international law, with critics fearing civilian harm and supporters citing military necessity.

title: 'Shanti Kranti': Where to stream Rajinikanth and Nagarjuna's film online
content: Back in 1991, the film industry was electrified by 'Shanti Kranti', a daring multi-lingual venture that saw Rajinikanth and Nagarjuna embody the same character in various linguistic versions. This high-stakes action crime drama, boasting a lavish budget and an impressive cast including Juhi Chawla and Khushbu, unfortunately did not resonate with audiences and became a box office disaster.

title: Tamil Nadu polls: No Brahmin candidate fielded by major parties
content: For the first time in 35 years, AIADMK has not fielded any Brahmin candidates in Tamil Nadu polls, reflecting the community's waning influence. Major parties like DMK, Congress, and BJP also lack Brahmin nominees, while TVK and NTK have fielded candidates, targeting constituencies with significant Brahmin votes.

title: 'Her dignity matters more': Retired judge greets divorced daughter with band-baaja in UP
content: In a bold statement against societal norms, a retired Meerut judge, Gyanendra Kumar Sharma, threw a heartwarming welcome party for his daughter, Pranita Vashistha, after her divorce. Embracing her return, he and family members donned shirts proclaiming 'I Love My Daughter' as they greeted her with festive drums and sweets.

title: Unusual summer temperature spike in Rayalaseema
content: According to data from the India Meteorological Department (IMD) and local observations, several places are already recording maximum temperatures between 39C and 41.5C

title: Assembly elections LIVE: PM Modi to address three rallies in Assam
content: The TMC accused the BJP of being involved in the unrest in Malda, where several judicial officers engaged in scrutiny of under-adjudication voters were gheraoed

title: Kerala likely to see 20% drop in remittances as West Asia war drags on
content: Although the depreciation of the currency against the dollar is expected to cushion short-term impact, Kerala is expected to see at least a 20% decline in remittances this year, especially if the war continues.The ramifications of this crisis could ripple through Kerala’s economy, which heavily relies on these funds.

title: Assam Election 2026: What BJP and Congress promise in their manifestos
content: BJP released its 31 point “Sankalp Patra” and Congress leader Rahul Gandhi, State party president Gaurav Gogoi and other senior leaders released the “Raijor Istahar”, or people manifesto in Assam.

title: Explore use of snakes and crocodiles in border rivers, BSF tells units
content: Parts of the Bangladesh border are prone to floods and the topography makes it difficult to erect fences

title: What does law say on tenure of Prime Minister?
content: India has developed a convention limiting a third presidential term, but the Constitution places no such restriction on the Prime Minister’s tenure. With Prime Minister Narendra Modi completing 8,931 days in office, this asymmetry invites closer scrutiny.

title: Kerala Assembly polls 2026: In Kazhakuttam, a three-cornered battle unfolds as pan-Kerala and local issues collide
content: BJP’s V. Muraleedharan aims to address local development needs, while incumbent CPI(M) MLA Kadakampally Surendran defends his record amid scrutiny from a gold theft scandal. Congress candidate T. Sarathchandra Prasad seeks to regain influence for his party in this diverse electorate, where infrastructure and community concerns are paramount.

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: Tight three-way contest keeps all camps on edge in Aranmula
content: One of Kerala’s largest constituencies is witnessing a sharply fought battle among LDF’s sitting MLA Veena George, UDF’s youth leader Abin Varkey, and NDA’s Kummanam Rajasekharan

title: West Asia crisis: Export uncertainty hits mango trade, Karnataka farmers explore alternative and local markets
content: Mangoes have already started arriving in Bengaluru, but traders say supplies have not yet reached full capacity. In the next 10 days, more varieties from Andhra Pradesh, Tamil Nadu and parts of Karnataka are expected to arrive

title: Kerala Assembly polls 2026: All major fronts hopeful of a win in Nemom
content: NDA’s Rajeev Chandrasekhar and UDF’s  K. S. Sabarinadhan to face incumbent MLA and LDF candidate V. Sivankutty in a high-stakes, three-way battle in the Nemom Assembly constituency

title: Kerala Assembly polls 2026 | Interviews with key candidates and political leaders
content: A collection of interviews by ‘The Hindu’ with key candidates and political leaders as part of Kerala Assembly elections 2026 coverage

title: AI is rewiring the world's most prolific film industry in India
content: Studios in India are responding by deploying AI at a scale unseen elsewhere: creating full-fledged AI-generated films; using AI dubbing to release movies in numerous languages; and recutting endings of older titles to eke out additional sales

title: West Asia crisis: Jaishankar speaks to Qatari PM, UAE Foreign Minister
content: In the last couple of weeks, India has made diplomatic efforts focusing on ending the conflict in West Asia as soon as possible and ensuring the unimpeded flow of energy through the Strait of Hormuz

title: KKCCI flags commercial LPG shortage, seeks assured supply for hotels in Kalyana Karnataka
content: Stakeholders emphasised that the commercial sector plays a crucial role in supporting livelihoods and catering to the needs of people

title: South Central Railway surpasses Railway Board targets in Kavach, ABS implementation
content: General Manager Sanjay Kumar Srivastava complimented the Signal and Telecommunications wing for the record commissioning of ABS and completion of Kavach field trials, stating that railway safety continues to remain the top priority.

title: Five cases of children falling victim to online games registered in Karnataka in past four years
content: The government of Karnataka plans to make it mandatory for gaming platforms to obtain licences and remove unauthorised apps

title: From selfies to slogan: how Instagram is becoming India’s newest political battleground
content: In the run-up to Assembly elections this month, social media is flooded with viral memes and AI-generated disinformation, and it is reshaping political speech, influence and visibility online

title: BJP plotted Malda unrest, PM should stop peddling falsehood: TMC
content: Senior TMC leader and State Education Minister Bratya Basu accused the PM of spreading hatred towards the Bengali language

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: FCRA amendment is an attack on minorities, says Kharge
content: He says the proposed amendment is not merely a legal change but a targeted strike against the Christian community; the AICC president claims a mutual understanding between LDF and BJP in State ahead of polls

title: Over 26,000 children under sponsorship scheme unpaid for a year in Karnataka
content: 26,767 orphaned, vulnerable or single-parent children have been registered under the scheme of the Women and Child Development Department

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: Broad Left platform involving former CPI(M) leaders in the works, says K.K. Rema
content: The Revolutionary Marxist Party leader says all the circumstances are conducive for a consecutive second victory for her in Vadakara and the campaign is mainly focussed on the anti-incumbency feelings against the Left Democratic Front government

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: Pinarayi claiming credit for Union govt. projects, says Amit Shah
content: Union Home Minister says Pinarayi has launched a ‘name change start-up’ by taking credit for Centre’s free ration and housing schemes

title: Kerala Assembly elections 2026: Tamil-speaking electorate holds key to Devikulam constituency
content: The Devikulam constituency will feature a notable “battle of Rajas,” with candidates F. Raja from the UDF, A. Raja from the LDF, and S. Rajendran of the NDA. Rajendran’s shift to the BJP could disrupt traditional voting patterns, while local residents express concerns over unresolved issues like housing and healthcare.

title: A debate on the tenure of Election Commission-appointed police chief
content: However, the EC order did not specify a tenure for him. The question is whether Mr. Rathore would be entitled to a minimum two-year tenure, as mandated by the Supreme Court in the  landmark Prakash Singh case. However, this ruling applies only to appointments made through the UPSC-led process

title: Kerala Assembly polls 2026: UDF fails to keep its promises: M.V. Govindan
content: Says it is natural for people to question how those who have mishandled disaster relief funds would handle the State’s administration if they come to power

title: India sends emergency relief supplies to flood and earthquake-hit Afghanistan
content: External Affairs Ministry spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal said India is committed to extending humanitarian assistance to the Afghan people in this challenging time

title: Ganga water sharing, visas, energy cooperation likely on agenda for Bangladesh FM visit
content: Visit likely on April 7 and 8; first FM visit since fall of Hasina regime; newly elected BNP indicates more willingness to negotiate Ganga water sharing treaty renewal, compared to the interim government

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: Personalities and political loyalties to define poll outcome in central Kerala
content: The Left Democratic Front had a clear upper hand in the region comprising Ernakulam, Idukki, Kottayam and Alappuzha in 2021 as it bagged 25 of the 37 Assembly seats. But this time, the shifting of political loyalty of some candidates and the fielding of some prominent individuals in some key constituencies have made the contests worth watching.

title: New Delhi sees flood of migrants returning home owing to LPG crisis
content: Rush at New Delhi Railway Station as workers head to Bihar and Uttar Pradesh amid supply delays and black market prices

title: Kiran Mazumdar-Shaw flags poor condition of Hosur Road; NHAI responds
content: The National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) responded to her on X, saying the stretch was completed in 2010 and has since seen rapid urban and industrial growth.

title: Case filed against AIMIM MLC and others for obstructing GHMC anti-encroachment drive
content: The complainant alleged that the group behaved aggressively, created a disturbance at the site and interfered with the demolition drive

title: ‘Some people are more native, but it’s a question of citizenship’: Hiren Gohain
content: As ‘infiltration’ makes yet another inevitable entry into the Assam campaign, the writer-civil society activist says the term ‘Miya’ was never a part of public rhetoric before

title: Assembly elections updates: Gherao of judicial officers a reflection of TMC’s ‘maha jungle raj’, says PM Modi
content: Rahul Gandhi said that Assam CM Himanta Biswa Sarma is India’s most corrupt Chief Minister and added, “He spreads only hatred”

title: Maharashtra Congress set to overhaul organisation in ‘Sangathan Srujan Abhiyan’
content: As part of the exercise, the party is finalising the list of 72 district presidents across the State, and in a departure from past practices, the campaign will also include appointments at the taluka level, covering 675 talukas

title: Dhanushkodi Athithan, a Congress veteran, calls for making the party a people’s movement
content: Dhanuskodi Athithan, a long-standing Congress leader and former Union Minister of State for Youth Affairs and Sport, wants the party to do field work and take up people’s issues. “Look at Tirunelveli and Kanniyakumari districts where the party is still strong. This is because of this approach,” he points out

title: T.N. Assembly election 2026: Despite a slew of infrastructure projects, Poonamallee constituency residents point to gaps in civic amenities
content: They demand a functional underground sewer system, motorable interior roads, and reliable drinking water supply; despite housing the Chembarambakkam lake, the area continues to face water supply issues; DMK MLA Krishnaswamy says most poll promises have been met

title: AIADMK hopes to take advantage of ‘anti-incumbency’, solid vote base in coastal districts
content: Of the six Assembly segments in Nagapattinam and Mayiladuthurai districts, the party had lost by a narrow margin in four constituencies in 2021 elections and won Vedaranyam seat

title: Govt. keen to resolve pending issues of employees: Atchannaidu
content: Andhra Pradesh Agriculture Minister Atchannaidu said employees’ role was critical in meeting the people’s aspirations

title: Akash Vishwanath More of Congress to contest Baramati Bypoll
content: The seat was vacant after the constituency’s elected MLA and the deputy Chief Minister of the State Ajit Pawar died tragically in a plane crash

title: T.N. Assembly election: Concerned over stalled growth, Ambattur constituency residents demand GH, sewer network, wider roads
content: Residents want an additional platform on the southern side of the Ambattur railway station for express and fast local trains towards Chennai to halt; they demand a government hospital as people have to go to Kilpauk Medical College Hospital or Stanley Hospital in case of emergencies

title: Development, welfare, and civic problems shape the poll mood in Thiru. Vi. Ka. Nagar
content: Residents say their struggle for basic amenities in some areas still persist; night food stalls and commercial activity have encroached road space and worsened traffic congestion; those living in railway lands in Rajiv Gandhi Nagar and Mangalapuram are also seeking alternate housing

title: Davangere South: Close contest on the cards while minorities are divided over supporting Shamanur family
content: As the campaigning for the bypoll is nearing its conclusion, the decision of the minorities who are divided over supporting Shamanur family, will decide whether the family’s sway over Davangere continues.
